title: ecbolium geniculatum
intro: Ecbolium geniculatum, also known as the hooked calumba or hooked dodder, is a species of flowering plant in the family Scrophulariaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and inflammation. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating skin conditions, and aiding in digestion. In herbal preparations, ecbolium geniculatum is often used in the form of a decoction or infusion, and its roots are also used to make a powder or tincture for medicinal purposes.
